document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education - Home Science Curriculum

sub_strands.name: 2.1 Meaning of Food and Nutrition
specific_learning_outcomes: Define food and nutrition
suggested_learning_experiences: Discussion on the definitions of food and nutrition.

sub_strands.name: 2.2 Classification of Foods
specific_learning_outcomes: Classify foods according to energy-giving, body-building, and protective functions
suggested_learning_experiences: Group learners into three groups; each group classifies foods based on function.
Present findings using a chart.

sub_strands.name: 2.3 Nutrients and Their Functions
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify the six major nutrients: carbohydrates, proteins, fats and oils, vitamins, minerals, and water
State the functions of each nutrient
List sources of each nutrient
suggested_learning_experiences: Learners work in groups to research and present on one nutrient using charts or digital presentations.
Create flashcards for nutrients, their functions, and sources.

sub_strands.name: 2.4 Balanced Diet
specific_learning_outcomes: Define a balanced diet
Explain the importance of a balanced diet
Construct a balanced meal using locally available foods
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyze sample daily meals and assess their balance.
Plan and present a balanced menu for a day.

sub_strands.name: 2.5 Methods of Cooking
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe various methods of cooking: boiling, steaming, roasting, baking, grilling, frying, etc.
Demonstrate appropriate cooking methods for different foods
Observe safety during cooking
suggested_learning_experiences: Oral Q&A to review previous lesson.
Discussion, demonstration, group work, and practical sessions.
Learners observe appearance and taste of cooked foods.
lesson_objectives: Identify foods that can be boiled.
evaluation_methods: Check whether lesson ended on time

steaming.definition: Cooking food using steam from boiling water, often in a container placed over boiling water.

roasting.definition: Cooking food near an intense source of heat such as in an oven, over charcoal, or a three-stone fire.
advantages: Oven can cook multiple foods at once.
Surface hardens, sealing in juices and tenderizing food.

core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 2.9 Nutrition Disorders
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify common nutrition disorders: kwashiorkor, marasmus, rickets, scurvy, obesity, anemia
Explain causes and symptoms of each disorder
Suggest preventive measures
suggested_learning_experiences: Research and present case studies on nutrition disorders.
Discuss dangers of anemia during pregnancy: difficulty carrying pregnancy, complications during birth, poor breastfeeding, newborn anemia.

sub_strands.name: 2.10 Non-Communicable Diseases
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify non-communicable diseases related to diet: diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, hypertension, cancer
Discuss contributing factors: tobacco use, physical inactivity, harmful alcohol use, unhealthy diets
Explain effects: increased healthcare costs, restricted ability to work, financial strain on households

asthma.definition: A condition where airways narrow, swell, and produce extra mucus.

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Fibres and Fabrics
specific_learning_outcomes: Differentiate between natural and synthetic fibres
Identify sources of various fibres
Describe properties of different fabrics
suggested_learning_experiences: Examine fabric samples and classify them by fibre type.
Conduct burn tests to identify fibres (if safe and supervised).

sub_strands.name: 3.2 Care of Clothes and Household Articles
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify appropriate methods for cleaning different fabrics
Demonstrate stain removal techniques
Practice correct ironing and storage methods
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 3.3 Sewing Tools and Equipment
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify sewing tools and equipment
Demonstrate proper handling and care of sewing tools
suggested_learning_experiences: Display and name various sewing tools.
Practice threading needles, winding bobbins, and machine operation.

sub_strands.name: 3.4 Basic Sewing Techniques
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate basic hand and machine stitches
Perform simple repairs and alterations on garments
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice running stitch, backstitch, hemming stitch, etc.
Sew on buttons, mend tears.

sub_strands.name: 3.5 Pattern Drafting and Garment Construction
specific_learning_outcomes: Take body measurements accurately
Interpret commercial patterns and symbols
Construct a simple garment from a pattern
body_measurements: Outer arm length (shoulder to waist with elbow bent)
Trouser outer length (crotch to desired length)
Crotch depth (sitting position, waist to chair top)

pattern_symbols.cutting_line: Outermost bold continuous line, 1.5 cm from fitting line, marked with scissors symbol.
pattern_symbols.notches_balance_marks: Marks along cutting line to match pieces together. Used at gathers, darts, shoulder seams, collar points, sleeve heads.
pattern_symbols.straight_grain: Solid line with arrows at both ends, parallel to selvedge/warp grain.
pattern_symbols.place_on_fold: Line with bent arrows toward edge; indicates fabric should be folded and cut on fold without cutting the fold.
pattern_symbols.flow_direction_arrows: Small arrows on seam lines showing stitching direction to prevent stretching.
pattern_symbols.seam_allowance: Distance between cutting line and fitting line.

sub_strands.name: 3.6 Laundry Practices
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe the steps in laundry: sorting, soaking, washing, rinsing, drying, ironing, storing
Use appropriate detergents and methods for different fabrics

detergents.meaning: Substances that assist in removing dirt.
how_they_work: Reduce water's surface tension for better penetration.

soap_detergents.made_from: Reaction between fats/oils (animal or plant) and alkalis (sodium/potassium hydroxide).

basins_buckets_karais: Avoid prolonged contact with boiling water.

core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Home Environment
specific_learning_outcomes: Describe features of a healthy home environment
Identify hazards in the home and school

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Ventilation
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the importance of ventilation
Identify methods of ventilating a room
importance: May lead to health complications from kitchen heat.
Smoke from fires irritates eyes and may cause bronchitis.

natural.description: Through openings like windows, doors, vents, chimneys.
natural.features: Louvre windows (slanting, stay open when raining), fanlights (small, high up), vertically pivoted windows.

sub_strands.name: 4.3 Lighting
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify sources of lighting in the home
Demonstrate safe use of lighting appliances
suggested_learning_experiences: Compare natural and artificial lighting.
Discuss safety with electrical and flame-based lights.

sub_strands.name: 4.4 Furnishing and Interior Decoration
specific_learning_outcomes: Select appropriate furnishings for different rooms
Apply principles of interior decoration
suggested_learning_experiences: Design a room layout using sketches or digital tools.
Discuss how color, texture, and space affect mood.

sub_strands.name: 4.5 Cleaning the Home
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify cleaning agents and equipment
Demonstrate correct cleaning methods for different surfaces
Practice improvisation of cleaning tools

care: Wash occasionally in hot soapy water.

improvisation: Maize husks tied to a stick

care: Occasionally wash in hot soapy water.
improvisation: Old cloths tied to a stick

care: Wash occasionally in hot soapy water.
Don’t keep acid substances in enamel items for long.

summarizing_methods: Observing and identifying specific items (e.g., glass, wood)
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 4.6 Time and Energy Management
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the importance of managing time and energy
Apply methods to conserve time and energy in daily tasks
importance: Allows smooth transition between activities without backtracking.
conservation_methods: Use a hatch/window between kitchen and dining for serving.

sub_strands.name: 4.7 Waste Management
specific_learning_outcomes: Classify household refuse
Identify appropriate disposal methods

sub_strands.name: 4.8 Water and Sanitation
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify sources and uses of water in the home
Practice safe water storage and usage
Maintain hygiene in water systems
sink_care: Disinfect with boiling water, salt, or disinfectant.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 4.9 Fuels and Energy
specific_learning_outcomes: Identify common fuels used in the home
Discuss safety precautions when using different fuels

sub_strands.name: 5.2 Principles of Child Growth and Development
5.4 Play and Learning Activities for Children
6.1 Citizenship
specific_learning_outcomes: Define citizenship
Explain rights and responsibilities of citizens
sub_strands.citizenship_definition: Being a member of a state or nation who owes allegiance to it and is entitled to its protection.

sub_strands.name: 6.2 Democracy
specific_learning_outcomes: Define democracy
Explain principles of democracy
sub_strands.democracy_definition: A system of government where power is vested in the people, exercised directly or through elected representatives.
sub_strands.integration: Is democratic and advocates for harmonious relationships in society.

sub_strands.name: 6.3 Human Rights
specific_learning_outcomes: Define human rights
Identify basic human rights
Discuss ways of promoting and protecting human rights
sub_strands.human_rights_definition: Rights inherent to all human beings, regardless of race, sex, nationality, religion, etc.
sub_strands.integration: Refers to fair treatment and promotion of equity, based on human dignity, solidarity, and elimination of inequalities.

sub_strands.name: 6.4 Gender
specific_learning_outcomes: Define gender
Differentiate between gender and sex
Discuss gender equality and equity
sub_strands.gender_definition: Socially constructed roles, behaviors, activities, and attributes that a society considers appropriate for men and women.
sub_strands.integration: Accords equal opportunities, fosters fairness and justice, shares resources equitably.

sub_strands.name: 6.5 Drug and Substance Abuse
specific_learning_outcomes: Differentiate between medicine, drugs, and substances
Explain drug use, abuse, and misuse
Identify signs and symptoms of drug abuse
Discuss measures to control misuse of medicine

definitions.medicine: Chemical/compound used to cure, halt, or prevent disease (e.g., painkillers).
definitions.drugs: Chemical compounds that change how the body and mind work (e.g., alcohol).
definitions.substances: Materials with intoxicating effects (e.g., cigarettes, alcohol).

drug_use_patterns.abuse: Used in a way not intended, more than prescribed (e.g., 2x3 taken as 3x2).
drug_use_patterns.misuse: Incorrect use, such as wrong dose, wrong time, stopping too soon, taking someone else’s prescription.

usefulness_of_medicine_box_info: Provides summary of benefits and side effects.
measures_to_control_misuse: Do not stop/change dosing without consulting doctor.
Never give your prescription to others.
Store prescription stimulants, sedatives, and opioids safely.
assignments: Collect empty medicine boxes and discuss the usefulness of the information provided.

sub_strands.name: 7.1 Care of the Sick at Home
specific_learning_outcomes: Define home-based care
Identify forms of assistance given to the sick
Meet nutritional needs of the sick
Provide personal care for bedridden patients
sub_strands.home_based_care_definition: Assistance given to the unwell while at home, including material, financial, and emotional support.
nutritional_needs: Prepare food in easy-to-eat forms (e.g., minced meat).
personal_care: Ensure patient is clean and comfortable.
Raise patient to position pan, then rest comfortably after use.
Ensure clear passage for wheelchair users.
Lower beds for easy access for diabetic or mobility-impaired patients.
Keep syringes accessible and dispose of needles immediately.

sub_strands.name: 7.2 Post-Natal Care
specific_learning_outcomes: Define post-natal care
Explain its importance
Describe services offered at post-natal clinics
sub_strands.post_natal_care_definition: Care given to the mother and baby after birth, provided at home and clinics.
importance: Ensures recovery of mother and health of baby.
clinic_services: Advise on nutrition, cleanliness, and childcare.

sub_strands.name: 7.3 Role Play in Home-Based Care
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate care procedures through role play
suggested_learning_experiences: Trainees role-play scenarios involving care for the sick, elderly, or disabled.

sub_strands.name: 8.1 Lesson Planning for Practical Subjects
specific_learning_outcomes: Develop a lesson plan for a Home Science practical lesson
Integrate theory and practice effectively

sub_strands.name: 8.2 Demonstration Techniques
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate correct procedures clearly and safely
Sequence demonstrations logically
Engage learners during demonstrations

sub_strands.name: 8.3 Learner-Centered Activities
specific_learning_outcomes: Organize group work and collaborative learning
Facilitate learner participation and inquiry

sub_strands.name: 8.4 Assessment in Practical Lessons
specific_learning_outcomes: Use diverse methods to assess practical skills and knowledge
Provide constructive feedback
